title: Public Safety / Scanner / Claim Consistency Gate
context: GrantLayer is publicly available on GitHub at https://github.com/Discodone/grantlayer.git in a Developer Preview / controlled-pilot posture (GL-176). GL-195 creates a consolidated public safety, scanner, and claim consistency gate for the current public snapshot after GL-187 through GL-194. This is a review/docs/test artifact only.

scanner_rule_review.description: Review of scanner categories applicable to the GrantLayer public snapshot. Separates true blockers from expected non-blocking false positives.
scanner_rule_review.blocker_evaluation: No blocker findings identified in the current public snapshot.
scanner_rule_review.false_positive_evaluation: Five FP classes documented. All are expected and non-blocking.

scanner_blocker_categories: raw secrets or secret material (actual API keys, Bearer tokens, passwords, PEM private keys)
real API keys or tokens or passwords
private customer data (real names, addresses, identifiers, emails, phone numbers)
private absolute paths in public-facing docs
exploit details in public docs or issues
instruction to push internal repo directly to GitHub
public claim that production SaaS is ready
public claim that tenant isolation is implemented

scanner_false_positive_categories.description: Safety rules containing keywords like 'do not include secrets' — contains the keyword but is not actual secret material.
scanner_false_positive_categories.why_non_blocking: The text prohibits the material rather than including it.
scanner_false_positive_categories.becomes_blocking_if: The surrounding context changes to include actual secret material.

scanner_false_positive_categories.description: FAQ questions like 'Is tenant isolation implemented?' answered 'No' or 'Not implemented'.
scanner_false_positive_categories.why_non_blocking: The answer correctly states the caveat; the phrase is present as documentation of absence, not presence.
scanner_false_positive_categories.becomes_blocking_if: The answer changes to 'Yes' or the caveat is removed.

scanner_false_positive_categories.description: The gate document and JSON artifact contain the prohibited-phrase patterns as documentation. Scanners will match their own definitions.
scanner_false_positive_categories.why_non_blocking: Meta-document containing definitions is not subject to the same rules as entry-point docs.
scanner_false_positive_categories.becomes_blocking_if: Pattern definitions move into executable deployment code or config.

scanner_false_positive_categories.description: GL-177/178/179/180 governance docs contain historical pre-public phrases and are explicitly excluded from the public snapshot (GL-181).
scanner_false_positive_categories.why_non_blocking: These files are not published to GitHub. The exclusion list is authoritative.
scanner_false_positive_categories.becomes_blocking_if: Those docs are un-excluded from the public snapshot and published.

scanner_false_positive_categories.description: Old scope-guard tests that fail because newer GL issue files exist (e.g. GL-186 scope-guard tests did not anticipate GL-195 files).
scanner_false_positive_categories.why_non_blocking: Pre-existing known failures; the files being flagged are in the allowed scope.
scanner_false_positive_categories.how_to_record: FP, pre-existing known issue. Record count in final report.
scanner_false_positive_categories.becomes_blocking_if: A scope-guard test fails for a file in an actively prohibited scope.

stale_phrase_rules.phrase: public GitHub release has not happened

stale_phrase_rules.reason_stale: Repository is public; no approval required to access.

stale_phrase_rules.phrase: if and when public publication is approved
stale_phrase_rules.reason_stale: Publication has occurred; this condition is already satisfied.

required_public_caveats: synthetic/demo data only — no real customer data
no real secrets — all tokens are placeholders
no real private grants or institutional data
security-sensitive reports route to GitHub Security Advisories
prohibited_public_claims: safe for real grant or institutional data
official SDK or package published (if not actually published)
hidden or stale publication-pending claims after repo is public

required_public_caveats: synthetic/demo data only — no real customer data
no real secrets — all tokens are placeholders
no real private grants or institutional data
security-sensitive reports route to GitHub Security Advisories
prohibited_public_claims: safe for real grant or institutional data
official SDK or package published (if not actually published)
hidden or stale publication-pending claims after repo is public

public_snapshot_safety_assessment.rationale: No real customer names, addresses, identifiers, or PII found in reviewed files. All examples use synthetic identifiers.
No real API keys, tokens, passwords, or private keys found. All tokens are placeholders.
No internal hostnames, Forgejo URLs, internal VM IPs, or internal absolute paths found in public-facing entry docs.
All four entry-point docs contain all required caveats. No prohibited claims found.
No stale public-state phrases found in public entry docs. Historical governance docs containing stale phrases are excluded from the public snapshot (GL-181).
Pre-existing scope-guard FPs in the full test suite are documented. This gate doc itself contains scanner-pattern-definitions FPs. Both are expected and non-blocking.
public_snapshot_safety_assessment.recommended_follow_up: Document per-FP class; monitor for new FP classes.

public_snapshot_safety_assessment.rationale: SECURITY.md section 2 routes security-sensitive reports to GitHub Security Advisories. GL-192 templates include explicit advisory routing.
GL-194 public publish used the clean snapshot workflow. No force push. No direct internal push to GitHub. No visibility change.
scripts/verify-first-output.sh produced MATCH. diff -u examples/grant_lifecycle_evidence_bundle.json /tmp/grantlayer_gl195_grant_lifecycle_check.json produced empty diff.
Developer Preview, not production SaaS, tenant isolation not implemented appear in README.md, AGENTS.md, llms.txt, SECURITY.md, all issue templates.
Not claimed / not provided for production SaaS in all entry docs. No production-ready claim present.
public_snapshot_safety_assessment.recommended_follow_up: GL-199 will consolidate the gap report.

public_snapshot_safety_assessment.rationale: Not implemented for tenant isolation in all entry docs.
public_snapshot_safety_assessment.recommended_follow_up: GL-199 will consolidate the isolation gap analysis.

findings.summary: This gate document itself contains scanner-pattern-definition text that will trigger keyword scanner heuristics.
findings.evidence: The Stale Public-State Phrase Rules and Scanner Blocker Categories sections contain exact phrases that scanner heuristics search for.
findings.recommended_action: Mark as expected FP. Scanners processing this file should treat it as a meta-document and exclude it from blocker evaluation.
findings.recommended_issue: No new issue required — document in scanner log and test suite.

findings.summary: Pre-existing scope-guard test failures in the full test suite occur because older GL scope-guard tests did not anticipate newer GL issue files.
findings.evidence: Full suite historically shows 9–21 pre-existing scope-guard false-positive failures, documented in GL-162D, GL-181, GL-186, GL-191, GL-192, GL-193, GL-194 histories.
findings.recommended_action: Accept as pre-existing known FPs. Record in final report. Do not fix by modifying historic test files.
findings.recommended_issue: GL-199 or a dedicated housekeeping issue can revisit if FP count grows significantly.

findings.summary: llms.txt Next Steps still references GL-193 as an upcoming issue, but GL-193 is complete.
findings.evidence: llms.txt line 79: 'Upcoming issues: GL-193 (public agent/API walkthrough refresh).' GL-193 was merged and published.
findings.recommended_action: Update llms.txt Next Steps to reference GL-195–GL-199 roadmap items in a subsequent issue.

findings.summary: No formal production readiness gap report v2 exists. Non-blocking for developer preview.
findings.evidence: README and SECURITY.md list individual caveats; no single consolidated production gap report v2 exists. GL-194 confirmed this gap.
findings.recommended_action: GL-199 Production Readiness Gap Report v2 should document all remaining hardening gates.

findings.summary: No formal tenant isolation gap analysis exists documenting what isolation work remains. Non-blocking.
findings.evidence: All four entry-point docs state tenant isolation is not implemented. No doc maps the isolation implementation path.
findings.recommended_action: Include tenant isolation gap analysis in GL-199 Production Readiness Gap Report v2.

decision_rationale: No blocker findings identified. No private data, secrets, or internal infrastructure found. No prohibited public claims. No stale public-state phrases in entry docs. No force-push or direct internal push. Security-sensitive reporting routed correctly. Both public examples match committed reference artifacts. Cautions: pre-existing scope-guard FPs are documented (GL-195-F002); this gate doc triggers scanner-pattern-definition FPs (GL-195-F001); llms.txt references a completed issue (GL-195-F003, low). Production readiness gap report v2 and tenant isolation gap analysis are planned for GL-199.
